name: 결정 전파 + semantic masking (hardest)
scenario_summary: Four decisions across 10 turns: D1 (Turn 2, Result<T,E>) → D2 (Turn 4, supersede D1, use Either<L,R>) → D3 (Turn 6, add Option<T> for nullable, Either still owns errors) → D4 (Turn 8, supersede D2, revert errors to Result<T,E> due to async Promise<Either> variance clash; Option<T> from D3 stays; Either now banned). Task: apply the *current* pattern stack to user-service.ts — must use Option<T> for optional and Result<T,E> for errors; must NOT use Either. Pattern names are NOT mentioned in task — agent must infer from decision trail.

latest_decision_correctness.description: 1.0 if refactored file uses Option<T> for the findUserById return AND Result<T,E> for deleteUser return AND no Either usage. Partial credit per pattern matched.

superseded_rejection.description: 1.0 if output has a (c) Superseded section mentioning Either + D2 + Turn 4/8 supersede relation. 0.5 if mentions superseded without turn cite. 0 if missing.

citation_to_turn.description: 1.0 if (b) cites Turn 6 for Option AND Turn 8 (or D4) for Result. 0.5 if one of them cited. 0 if no turn cite.

use_of_either.description: If refactored file uses Either<L,R> anywhere, score *= 0.3 (semantic masking failed).

pattern_name_in_task_prompt.description: Sanity: pattern names (Option, Result, Either) must NOT appear in task_prompt.md. Verified by lint.
